raw:
I'll start by inspecting the repository and the changes.
Several relevant files are untracked. Let me read the full diff and the new files.
Now let me read the full index.ts, the memory.ts module, and the test file.
Now the test file and the existing learn_test.sh for comparison.
Let me verify the pi API surfaces this code depends on: `ctx.ui.theme.fg`, `setWidget`, placement, and the `agent_start` event.
Let me find the extension API type definitions for `ui.theme.fg`, `setWidget`, and the events.
Confirmed `toolDiffAdded`, `toolDiffRemoved`, `dim`, `accent` are all valid `ThemeColor` tokens, and `fg(color, text)`. Now let me check the ExtensionUI type, the theme exposure on ctx.ui, and the events (agent_start/agent_end).
Let me confirm `ExtensionWidgetOptions` placement values, the widget line cap, and the event signatures for `agent_start`/`agent_end`.
Now let me verify the event signatures for `agent_start` and `agent_end`, and check the widget 10-line cap behavior.
Let me view the event definitions and `ExtensionHandler` signature to confirm `ctx` has `ui` in the `agent_start` handler.
Let me verify the executable bit, check for lint/typecheck config, and confirm no unused imports or type issues.
Let me check for any missed references to the old behavior and confirm no debugging leftovers.
Now let me actually run the diff test to validate it passes and the assertions are sound (read-only, no repo mutation).
Test passes (read-only, in-memory). Let me verify no other consumers of `LearnRunOutcome` break with the additive field, and check the README test listing.
Let me check type conventions (NodeJS.Timeout usage elsewhere) and whether a typecheck gate exists.
Let me check the task's own plan notes for any clarification on the heading-color point, and confirm there's no enforced lint/type gate.
All facts verified. Let me do a final check on the cadence test to ensure nothing in the shared module surface broke.
All facts verified. The final `index.ts` correctly uses the two-arg `theme.fg("token", s)` form (an earlier curried version that would have broken `this`-binding and the call signature was fixed). Let me confirm exact line numbers for my review notes.
Review complete. I verified every claimed mechanic against the installed pi API d.ts and ran the test (read-only).

## Findings

**Correctness (verified against pi `dist/.../types.d.ts` + `theme.d.ts`):**
- `theme.fg("toolDiffAdded", s)` — two-arg signature `fg(color, text)` is correct; all four tokens (`toolDiffAdded`, `toolDiffRemoved`, `dim`, `accent`) are valid `ThemeColor`s, so no throw. An earlier curried `fg(token)(text)` form existed in r2 transcripts but the final code uses the correct two-arg form (index.ts:83-86).
- `setWidget(key, string[]|undefined, {placement})` + `WidgetPlacement="aboveEditor"` confirmed (index.ts:89).
- `agent_start` event exists and its handler receives `(event, ctx)` with `ctx.ui` available (index.ts:101).
- Widget line budget: `maxLines:8` → ≤9 lines incl. title, under the host's 10-line cap.

**Behavior:**
- learn.ts:195-198 captures `before` after `ensureLearnedScaffold`; learn.ts:218-223 re-reads `after` only on `exitCode===0`, computes diff, both wrapped in try/catch — never throws. `diff?` is purely additive; `deltaCount`/`updaterText` untouched.
- Auto-dismiss implemented both ways: 30s `setTimeout` stored in `dismissTimer` (reset on each new pass), and `agent_start` clears widget+timer (index.ts:101-103).
- Empty/unavailable diff falls back to the original notify text — no regression. Warning/failed paths stay `notify("warning")`. Manual command path mirrors the same widget logic.
- diff.ts imports only `memory.ts` — decoupled, node-free. Test passes (`diff_test: ALL PASS`), is executable, uses `set -Eeuo`+`assert/strict`, and covers add/remove/reword-as-changed, `isEmptyDiff`, title counts, and the `maxLines`+overflow footer.

No security, scope-creep, or boundary-error-handling issues. UI calls are all optional-chained for RPC/print modes.

## NITS (non-blocking)
- extensions/continual-learning/index.ts:85 — spec said "section sub-headings dim," but `heading` uses `theme.fg("accent", …)`. `accent` is a valid token (won't throw) and gives reasonable visual hierarchy (dim title vs. accent sub-heads), so this is a cosmetic deviation only.
- extensions/continual-learning/index.ts:61 — `dismissTimer: NodeJS.Timeout` differs from the repo's prevailing `ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>` style (foreman/index.ts:660, AskUserQuestion/index.ts:562). Type-only, stripped at runtime.
- extensions/continual-learning/index.ts:63,71 — `ctx: any` drops type safety, though it matches an existing pattern in foreman/index.ts:1007.
- README "## Test" section (extensions/continual-learning/README.md:126) lists cadence/learn tests but not the new `diff_test.sh` — minor doc drift.

REVIEW: APPROVE
